PLANTED FIXTURE — do not "fix" this file.

Sole purpose: the CI self-test for scripts/qc-assert-no-client-names.sh copies
this file into an isolated temp repo-root and confirms the gate exits 1. It
deliberately contains an obviously-fake PLACEHOLDER name from the committed
roster template (scripts/client-roster.example.txt) AND the operator machine
path so the self-test proves the gate actually bites on BOTH categories —
without embedding any real client name.

The self-test points OPENCLAW_CLIENT_ROSTER at scripts/client-roster.example.txt
(which lists "ExampleClientAlpha"), so the gate loads that placeholder as its
denylist and must FAIL on the line below. Clean content (no placeholder, no
operator path) must PASS. This proves the detector without depending on the
operator's real roster.

This exact path is path-anchored-excluded from the gate's own scan (see the
_is_excluded predicate) because it holds the tokens as detection TEST DATA, not
as a real leak.

  const owner = "ExampleClientAlpha";
  const key   = "/Users/blackceomacmini/.ssh/id_ed25519";
